article_meta.title: PlanetOur Almost-Apocalyptic Climate FutureBy shooting for 3 degrees Celsius of warming, the world could slide toward a

reasoning: The model identifies several genuine bias instances in the author's own prose without flagging any quoted text. The identified instances are defensible - 'Almost-Apocalyptic,' 'ham-fisted,' 'merciless,' 'ritual in frustration and futility,' and the actuarial risk analogy are all author prose with clear bias patterns. However, the model misses several additional significant instances of author-level bias in this heavily opinionated article, such as 'If warming the planet beyond 1 degree Celsius isn't safe, then 3 degrees is madness' (Opinion Presented as Fact), 'the purveyors of this kind of pragmatism are downright oblivious' (Mudslinging/Ad Hominem or Mind Reading), 'one of the fastest derangements of the carbon cycle' (Word Choice - 'derangements'), and the overall Slant of the piece presenting climate catastrophism as unquestioned fact throughout the author's prose.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All five flagged items are from the author's own prose. The quoted text from André Corrêa do Lago and Matthew Huber was correctly excluded. No attribution rule violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All five flagged items are defensible bias instances in the author's own voice. 'Almost-Apocalyptic' in the headline, 'ham-fisted,' 'merciless/succumbs/fall away,' 'ritual in frustration and futility,' and the actuarial risk analogy are all genuinely loaded author language.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The article is heavily opinionated throughout the author's prose. Missed instances include: 'then 3 degrees is madness' (opinion as fact), 'downright oblivious' (ad hominem toward 'climate pragmatists'), 'derangements of the carbon cycle' (loaded word choice), 'industrial civilization erupts more carbon dioxide' (sensational verb), 'gavage of CO2' (loaded word choice implying force-feeding), and the overall slant presenting worst-case scenarios without balancing perspectives.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The labels applied are largely correct. 'Sensationalism/Emotionalism' for the apocalyptic headline and dramatic ocean collapse language is appropriate. 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives' for 'ham-fisted' is correct. 'Word Choice' for 'ritual in frustration and futility' is appropriate. All labels are defensible.
